title: Daily Market Digest: Tech Rout Continues Amidst Jobs Report Anticipation
subtitle: July 02, 2026

items.headline: U.S. Futures Mixed as Tech Sell-off Persists Ahead of Key Jobs Data
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are wavering on Thursday, July 2, as the tech-heavy Nasdaq anticipates further declines following a significant sell-off in chip and AI-related stocks on Wednesday. Investors are keenly focused on the imminent release of the June nonfarm payrolls report, a critical indicator for the Federal Reserve's future monetary policy. Despite the tech sector's struggles, Dow Jones futures show slight gains, indicating a rotation of investor interest.

sections.title: Major Indices Performance (as of July 1, 2026 close)

items.statContext: Down 0.7% (173.69 points) on Wednesday, halting a two-session gaining streak.
Marginally down 0.027% (13.96 points) on Wednesday after hitting a new intraday record.

sections.title: Top Movers (as of July 1, 2026 close / July 2, 2026 pre-market)

items.headline: Meta Platforms Surges on Cloud Business News
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) jumped nearly 9% on July 1 after reports emerged about its plans to launch a 'Meta Compute' cloud business, aiming to monetize its excess AI computing capacity.

items.headline: Tesla Sees Weekly Gains, Positive Q2 Outlook
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) was up 1.2% on July 1 and recorded a 12.0% weekly gain from June 26 to July 1, driven by the closure of a NHTSA safety probe and the rollout of FSD v14 Lite. Analysts are revising Q2 delivery forecasts upward.

items.headline: Micron Technology Leads Chipmaker Declines
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) plunged 10.6% on July 1 as investors took profits in AI-linked hardware amid a broader sell-off in semiconductor stocks.

items.headline: SanDisk Corp Falls Alongside Chip Sector
items.summary: SanDisk Corp ($SNDK) also dropped 10.6% on July 1, part of the widespread sell-off affecting chipmakers and memory stocks.

items.headline: Franklin Covey Drops on Lowered Guidance
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. ($FC) fell 19.69% in premarket trading on July 2 after the company lowered its full-year revenue guidance.

items.headline: CoreWeave Hit by Meta's Cloud Entry
items.summary: CoreWeave ($CRWV) experienced a 14% drop on July 1 following news of Meta Platforms' entry into the cloud computing market, directly impacting neo-cloud service providers.

items.headline: June Nonfarm Payrolls Report Eyed by Investors and Federal Reserve
items.summary: The highly anticipated June nonfarm payrolls report is set for release today, July 2. Economists project the U.S. economy to have added 115,000 jobs last month. This data will be crucial in shaping expectations for the Federal Reserve's interest rate path, with the next FOMC meeting scheduled for July 29.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Market Decline Amidst Profit-Taking
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductor and AI stocks, is experiencing significant weakness and profit-taking. Major players like Micron Technology, SanDisk Corp, AMD, and Intel saw substantial declines, pulling down the Nasdaq. This follows a strong first half of 2026 for the sector.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Pivots into Cloud Computing with 'Meta Compute'
items.summary: Meta Platforms is reportedly launching 'Meta Compute,' a new cloud business designed to sell its excess AI computing capacity to external customers. This strategic move aims to transform AI infrastructure spending from a cost center into a potential new revenue stream, impacting competitors in the cloud services market.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Approaches; Currys Reports Today
items.summary: While the broader Q2 earnings season officially kicks off in mid-July with major banks and tech giants, Currys ($CURY) is hosting its H2 2026 earnings call today, July 2. FactSet Research Systems Inc. ($FDS) also reported its Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings, highlighting growth driven by AI adoption.

items.headline: Easing Iran Tensions Impact Oil Prices
items.summary: Progress in indirect U.S.-Iran talks in Qatar has led to increased optimism about a broader diplomatic resolution, contributing to easing global energy supply fears. Brent crude oil prices remain under pressure, extending declines towards $70 a barrel.

items.headline: Market Sees Rotation Towards Cyclical Sectors
items.summary: Despite weakness in the tech sector, there's evidence of a 'Great Rotation' of funds into more traditional and cyclical sectors. Financials and Industrials are attracting inflows, helping to cushion the broader market.
